protein_name: May play a role in actin remodeling. Involved in the dissociation of the integrin-tensin-actin complex. EGF activates TNS4 and down-regulates TNS3 which results in capping the tail of ITGB1. Seems to be involved in mammary cell migration. May be involved in cell migration and bone development. Expressed in umbilical vein endothelial cells, epithelial cells, and fibroblasts cells (at protein level). Highly expressed in thyroid, kidney and placenta. Low expression in heart, skeletal muscle, spleen, liver, and lung. Expressed in tumor endothelial cells. Expression seems to be down-regulated in thyroid tumor tissues and in anaplastic carcinomas. 4 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Molecular Function: hydrolase activity; phosphoprotein phosphatase activity; protein binding
raw_fields.Biological Process: positive regulation of Rac protein signal transduction
